channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ben Murray-Bruce Reflects on the Collapse of His Concert Business After 1983 Coup
item.description: Ben Murray-Bruce, founder of Silverbird Group and former senator, recounted how the 1983 military coup in Nigeria, which removed President Shehu Shagari, devastated his concert promotion business. Lau...

item.title: PTA Teachers In Federal Unity Colleges Begin Nationwide Withdrawal Of Services Over Delayed Regularisation And Poor Pay
item.description: Na so e be say thousands of PTA part-time teachers wey dey work for Federal Unity Colleges all over Nigeria don start to withdraw their services from normal teaching activities. Dem dey protest di fed...

item.title: India and UAE Formalise Strategic Defence and Energy Cooperation
item.description: During Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s recent visit to the United Arab Emirates (UAE), India and the UAE finalised a framework for a strategic defence partnership. The agreement covers multiple areas, ...

item.description: Gloria Swanson was more than a Hollywood star; she was a model for modern celebrity, known for her dramatic reinvention from the silent film era of the 1920s to the sound era of the 1930s. In the 1920...

item.title: American influencer faces assault charge following London Tube incident
item.description: An American social media influencer has been formally charged with assault following an incident at a London Tube station. Details regarding the exact nature of the altercation have not been fully dis...

item.title: Jury Concludes Trial Between Altman and Musk Over AI Industry Dispute
item.description: The highly anticipated trial between Sam Altman and Elon Musk, key figures in the artificial intelligence industry, reached its final stages this week. Although full details of the proceedings remain ...

item.title: MP Osoro Faces Backlash at Onyonka's Mother's Funeral Over Ruto's Contribution

item.title: Low Data Usage of 1.6GB Monthly Hinders Tanzania Digital Vision 2050 Goals
item.description: According to the Tanzania Communications Regulatory Authority (TCRA), internet users in the country consumed an average of only 1.6GB of data per month as of December 2025. This equals about 55MB dail...

item.title: Cross River State IRS Seals Federal Institutions Over Outstanding Tax Liabilities

item.description: The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has issued a fresh weather advisory warning citizens about heavy rains, thunderstorms, and isolated hailstorms expected across many parts of Pakistan ...

item.title: UK faces record borrowing costs as political instability affects markets
item.description: The United Kingdom is experiencing a significant increase in borrowing costs, reaching levels not seen since the financial crisis era. This rise in interest rates for government debt reflects growing ...

item.title: Northern Kenya PWD Chairman Calls for Fuel Tax Relief as Prices Rise Sharply
item.description: Persons with disabilities in Kenya are feeling the heavy burden of the latest fuel price increase announced by EPRA. The new prices took effect on 15th May 2026, with petrol in Nairobi now at KSh 214....

item.description: Representative Steve Cohen, a Democrat who has served ten terms representing the Memphis area, announced that he will not seek re-election after Tennessee's Republican-controlled legislature redrew hi...

item.title: The Decline of Traditional Tight Swaddling Practices in Mid-20th Century Northern Italy
item.description: In Italian cultural history, particularly in the isolated valleys of northern regions like Trentino-Alto Adige and Friuli Venezia Giulia, a centuries-old custom known as “Born Bound” or “Nati Fasciati...
